title: Functools, Operator Functions, Caching, and Dispatch · Lazy Trace Forensics
summary: Build reusable scoring functions without obscuring cache validity or dispatch behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a measured callable utility and dispatch package and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use partial application to bind stable configuration without hiding required arguments.
Use cache and lru_cache only for hashable inputs with stable effect-free semantics.
Design singledispatch registrations around first-argument type contracts and safe fallbacks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Wrapper Metadata value-and-effect pipeline for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Wrapper Metadata assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.
